title: FAQ
subtitle: Things I get asked a lot

questions.label: Tell me about your background.
questions.content: I've spent 10+ years building systems across AI, backend, and hardware. I combine research thinking with practical delivery — designing systems that work in production, not just in theory. I started programming at 11, worked across the US and Japan, led engineering teams of 5–15 people, founded an R&D-driven AI company, and exited with profit.

questions.label: What kind of teams have you led?
questions.content: Teams of 5–15 engineers across AI, backend, and embedded systems — both inside established organizations and within my own company. I focused on architecture, system design, and aligning execution with real-world constraints.

questions.label: What happened with your company?
questions.content: I built an R&D-driven engineering company focused on AI and systems. We delivered real products, tested new ideas, and operated as a fast experimental environment. I exited by selling the intellectual property at a profit.

questions.label: What do you actually work with?
questions.content: I work across backend systems, AI integration, and automation — primarily using TypeScript, Python, and cloud infrastructure. I also have deep experience in embedded systems, computer vision, and hardware-integrated platforms when needed.

questions.label: What domains have you shipped production work in?
questions.content: Automation & data systems, AI-driven tools for classification and decision support, computer vision pipelines, real-time streaming, embedded systems (Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Jetson), and full-stack platforms across enterprise and startup environments.

questions.label: What work are you open to?
questions.content: System diagnosis & architecture design, AI and automation strategy, and high-impact consulting — short-term or recurring. Also open to contract and selective full-time roles where technical depth and ownership belong at the table.

questions.label: What languages do you speak?
questions.content: Portuguese (native), English (fluent), Japanese (business level, JLPT N2), and Spanish (conversational).
